frameworks.name: NMDA-dependent synaptic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: long-lasting cortical excitability changes mediated by NMDA receptor activation

frameworks.name: non-invasive brain stimulation
frameworks.one_line: weak scalp currents modulate underlying cortical activity

core_question: What are the immediate and after effects of anodal and cathodal tDCS on mouse primary somatosensory cortex, and what mechanism underlies the after effects?
key_assumptions: weak transcranial currents reach and modulate S1 neuronal activity
anodal and cathodal polarities produce opposite-sign neural effects
NMDA receptor pharmacology cleanly dissociates plasticity from acute modulation
key_findings: anodal tDCS increased spontaneous and evoked S1 activity during application
anodal tDCS produced long-lasting potentiation after stimulation
cathodal tDCS reduced both spontaneous and evoked activity
NMDA receptor block abolished the after effect
interpretations: NMDA-dependent plasticity is the mechanism underlying tDCS after effects
tDCS exerts dissociable acute modulation and lasting plasticity on sensory cortex
